page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8481C cores with hyper-threading. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance is equipped with 8 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor features a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this configuration.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside constrained multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves average results in single-core stress-ng and Geekbench tests, as well as single-threaded Gzip compression and Bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core execution scales poorly, with Geekbench multi-core and PassMark CPU scores falling into the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with uncached read speeds of 27.54 GB/sec and a PassMark memory score in the bottom 10%. Despite these memory bottlenecks, static web serving throughput remains stable at 6.78 GB/sec, and hardware-accelerated encryption tests perform at an average level.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-highcpu-4 offers a dedicated compute profile suitable for workloads that require predictable CPU allocation without the overhead of high memory capacities. The inclusion of AMX and AVX-512 instructions provides qualitative advantages for specialized vector math and lightweight machine learning inference, such as small-scale LLM text generation. This instance is best suited for single-threaded processing pipelines, basic web serving, lightweight containerized microservices, and cryptographic operations that do not demand high memory bandwidth or local disk I/O.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. It contains 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 105 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth reside in the lower percentiles. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which assist with vector calculations and cryptography. This instance provides a cost-effective option for dedicated compute tasks with low memory requirements. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing, and basic containerized microservices.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight dedicated compute workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-4 features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, offering balanced single-core performance for compute-bound tasks.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring Intel Xeon processors and advanced instruction sets for memory-lean, CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8481C processor at 2.7 GHz
Equipped with 8 GB of RAM and a large 105 MB L3 cache
Supports Intel AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets for vector and matrix math
Delivers average single-core performance and stable static web serving throughput
Configured without local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight web serving, microservices, and single-threaded tasks
